title: Calyptridium paniculatum
intro: Calyptridium paniculatum, also known as mountain king or panicled maireana, is a low-growing, succulent plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Calyptridium paniculatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Calyptridium paniculatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Montiaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships to other plants. Calyptridium paniculatum is native to North America, specifically the western regions, where it grows in dry areas. It is a member of the genus Calyptridium, which includes several species of annual and perennial plants. The classification of Calyptridium paniculatum reflects its unique features and adaptations to its environment.
uses: Calyptridium paniculatum is an alpine plant native to the western United States, and its medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge.  The plant's roots and stems are used to make a medicinal tea that is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.  Some herbalists also use Calyptridium paniculatum to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.  The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds.  Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Calyptridium paniculatum for medicinal purposes.
